plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate. Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to exit all leveraged ETFs and reduce risk. Add to QQQI to increase income and stability in the portfolio. Buy NOW as a high-scoring quality name for conservative growth. Build a 15% cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a target of ~1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and building a cash buffer, leading to lower potential drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate. Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce overall leverage and exit positions below their stops. Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single-name concentration. Buy ANET, a high-scoring tech name, for growth. Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. Leverage is reduced, and concentration is managed, offering a blend of growth potential with moderate risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate. Re-enter SOXL at 5% with a strict stop, leveraging high-scoring semiconductors. Sell SPAL to reallocate capital to stronger setups. Add to CRWD, pressing a winning position. Buy TECL and NOW, both high-scoring leveraged tech setups, to maximize growth potential.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It embraces higher leverage and concentration in high-scoring setups, which significantly raises both upside and potential drawdown. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, and strict stop discipline is crucial to manage rapid losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% and QQQ to 10% to reduce broad index exposure. Add significantly to QQQI to increase the income focus of the portfolio. Trim SMH and CRWD to reduce sector and high-beta single-name concentration. Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all leveraged ETFs. Maintain a 15% cash buffer for stability.

This plan aims to park cash for yield, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It focuses on income and broad index ETFs while minimizing leverage and high-beta names. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs; this is income, not a savings account.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It focuses on income and broad index ETFs while minimizing leverage and high-beta names. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs; this is income, not a savings account.
